mission.title: opentelemetry-collector: [service/telemetry/metrics] GRPC OTLP periodic reader ignores insecure configuration
mission.description: [service/telemetry/metrics] GRPC OTLP periodic reader ignores insecure configuration. This issue affects 11+ users.

steps.title: Identify opentelemetry-collector troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your opentelemetry-collector deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector -n opentelemetry-collector --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `code = DeadlineExceeded desc = context deadline exceeded`

steps.title: Review opentelemetry-collector configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentelemetry-collector configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector
kubectl get configmap -n opentelemetry-collector -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentelemetry-collector
```
### Component(s)

_No response_

### What happened?

OpenTelemetry Collector ignores both `insecure: true` and `http` schema in endpoint in internal telemetry periodic reader GRPC configuration.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [service/telemetry/metrics] GRPC OTLP periodic reader…
steps.description:
Issue is valid for both vanilla and contrib distributions.
Here is the log from `otelcol` distribution:
```
$ ./otelcol --config otel-grpc-test.yaml
2025-03-24T11:56:56.731+0200    info    service.122.1/service.go:193 Setting up own telemetry...
2025-03-24T11:56:56.731+0200    info   
```yaml
receivers:
  otlp:
    protocols:
      grpc:
        endpoint: localhost:4317

exporters:
  debug: {}

service:
  pipelines:
    metrics:
      receivers:
        - otlp
      exporters:
        - debug
  telemetry:
    logs:
        level: INFO
    metrics:
      level: normal
      readers:
        - periodic:
            interval: 10000
            # Timeout for reader itself, in milliseconds
            timeout: 3000
            exporter:
              otlp:
                protocol: grpc
                endpoint: http://localhost:4317
                compression: gzip
                ins
```

steps.title: Confirm [service/telemetry/metrics] GRPC OTLP periodic… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentelemetry-collector -n opentelemetry-collector --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n opentelemetry-collector --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `code = DeadlineExceeded desc = context deadline exceeded` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
Issue is valid for both vanilla and contrib distributions.
Here is the log from `otelcol` distribution:
```
$ ./otelcol --config otel-grpc-test.yaml
2025-03-24T11:56:56.731+0200    info    service.122.1/service.go:193 Setting up own telemetry...
2025-03-24T11:56:56.731+0200    info    builders/builders.go:26 Development component. May change in the future.
codeSnippets:
receivers:
  otlp:
    protocols:
      grpc:
        endpoint: localhost:4317

exporters:
  debug: {}

service:
  pipelines:
    metrics:
      receivers:
        - otlp
      exporters:
        - debug
  telemetry:
    logs:
        level: INFO
    metrics:
      level: normal
      readers:
        - periodic:
            interval: 10000
            # Timeout for reader itself, in milliseconds
            timeout: 3000
            exporter:
              otlp:
                protocol: grpc
                endpoint: http://localhost:4317
                compression: gzip
                insecure: true
                # Timeout for sending telemetry, in milliseconds
                timeout: 5000

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentelemetry-collector installed or the issue environment reproducible.
